title: Vachellia permixta
taxonomy: Vachellia permixta is classified under the family Fabaceae, which is also known as the legume family. This classification is due to the plant's characteristic fruit, which is a legume. Within the Fabaceae family, Vachellia permixta is further classified under the subfamily Caesalpinioideae. The genus Vachellia is a part of the Acacia family, but it has been reclassified from Acacia to Vachellia in some taxonomic systems. This complex classification reflects the ongoing research and reevaluation of plant taxonomy.
uses: Vachellia permixta, also known as the 'mixed thorn acacia', is a species of thorn tree native to the tropical regions of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and skin conditions.
